summary: Removed broad, incorrect include-path hacks from configure.py and repaired the resulting dependency fallout by making sysdolphin, Dolphin OS, placeholder, and one ftCommon source include the headers they actually depend on. The PR mostly converts baselib sources from relying on global <baselib/...> include visibility and transitive headers to local/self headers plus explicit local dependencies.
what_changed: Removed custom include/system_include additions from DolphinLib in configure.py, including the src/melee and src/sysdolphin HACK paths.

Removed the src/melee lbrefract hack and the duplicate sysdolphin system include from SysdolphinLib in configure.py while keeping src/sysdolphin as a normal include and src/dolphin as a system include.
Removed analogous src/melee and src/sysdolphin HACK paths from RuntimeLib in configure.py.
Moved OSReport and OSPanic declarations out of the umbrella src/dolphin/os.h and into src/dolphin/os/OSError.h, where OS error/panic handling declarations are more specific.
Added explicit missing includes in Dolphin OS sources, including OSError.h in OSThread.c, OSExi.h in OSUartExi.c, and OSCache.h under MUST_MATCH in __ppc_eabi_init.c.
Removed broad transitive includes from src/placeholder.h, notably baselib/forward.h and dolphin/os.h, and included OSError.h only where NOT_IMPLEMENTED needs OSPanic.
Converted many sysdolphin/baselib .c files to include their own header first or near the top, then local sibling headers such as aobj.h, debug.h, dobj.h, fog.h, jobj.h, list.h, object.h, etc.

Converted many baselib headers from angle-bracket <baselib/...> includes to quoted "baselib/..." includes, often marking forward.h with IWYU pragma: export when it intentionally re-exports forward declarations.

Reduced baselib header transitive dependencies by deleting unnecessary includes such as baselib/aobj.h, baselib/mobj.h, baselib/wobj.h, ft/forward.h, it/forward.h, dolphin/gx/forward.h, and others where the diff showed they were no longer needed.
Changed HSD_GObjList fields fighters and items in gobj.h from Fighter_GObj* and Item_GObj* to HSD_GObj*, removing the need for ft and item forward headers in that sysdolphin header.
Added an explicit sysdolphin/baselib/cobj.h include to ftCo_09F4.c after removing configure.py's broad src/sysdolphin visibility assumptions.
smart_moves: Fixed the root build-system problem instead of keeping hidden global include-path hacks, making incorrect dependencies visible at compile time.
Repaired the fallout by adding precise includes at use sites rather than restoring transitive umbrella headers.
Used local quoted includes for sysdolphin/baselib internals, matching the fact that these files are within the same library/module rather than external system headers.
Moved OSReport and OSPanic declarations to OSError.h, reducing the scope of dolphin/os.h and making panic/report users depend on the specific OS error interface.
Used IWYU pragma: export on baselib/forward.h in several public baselib headers, documenting that forward declarations are intentionally part of those headers' public include surface.
Removed fighter/item-specific forward dependencies from baselib/gobj.h by typing list slots as HSD_GObj*, which keeps sysdolphin less coupled to melee gameplay layers.
decomp_lessons: Avoid solving decomp include failures by adding broad include paths in configure.py; those paths can mask bad header ownership and accidental cross-library dependencies.
When removing include-path hacks, expect a wide but mechanical cleanup: each source should include the exact header that declares the symbols it uses.
Library-internal sysdolphin code should prefer local quoted includes such as "aobj.h" or "baselib/object.h" over relying on globally injected <baselib/...> lookup.
Umbrella headers like dolphin/os.h should not become dumping grounds for unrelated declarations; moving declarations to narrower headers improves dependency clarity.
Public headers may still intentionally re-export forward declarations, but that choice should be explicit, as shown by the IWYU pragma: export comments.
Reducing transitive includes in headers can force downstream files, such as ftCo_09F4.c, to state their real dependencies explicitly.
Changing overly specific cross-layer pointer typedefs to base sysdolphin types can remove unnecessary dependencies on gameplay-layer forward declarations.
naming_conventions: Baselib local includes use quoted paths, e.g. "aobj.h", "debug.h", "baselib/forward.h", rather than angle-bracket <baselib/...> includes after this cleanup.
Headers that intentionally expose baselib forward declarations commonly use "baselib/forward.h" with the comment "// IWYU pragma: export".
Dolphin OS specific headers follow the path form <dolphin/os/OSError.h>, <dolphin/os/OSExi.h>, <dolphin/os/OSCache.h>, and <dolphin/os/OSInit.h>.
The PR preserves existing HSD_* type naming, such as HSD_GObj, HSD_ObjAllocData, HSD_DObjInfo, and HSD_Archive.
The gobj list fields retain names fighters and items but are typed as HSD_GObj* instead of Fighter_GObj* and Item_GObj*.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly rewrite or function matching tactic was documented in comments or PR body.
One include addition was guarded by MUST_MATCH in __ppc_eabi_init.c, suggesting the dependency is only needed for matching/building that configuration.
The change is primarily a build/include-dependency cleanup, not a codegen or asm-matching PR.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available beyond the title and diff.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching configure.py include paths or removing remaining HACK include entries.
Search for remaining angle-bracket <baselib/...> includes that should be converted to quoted local includes.
Search for headers relying on transitive placeholder.h or dolphin/os.h declarations instead of specific Dolphin OS headers.
Audit remaining sysdolphin/baselib headers for unnecessary gameplay-layer dependencies such as ft/forward.h or it/forward.h.
Check whether the placeholder.h non-GNU NOT_IMPLEMENTED branch compiles as intended after the OSError include/path change.
